name: coercer
title: coercer

commands.name: coercer

arguments.description: show this help message and exit
arguments.raw: -h, --help          show this help message and exit

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# coercer -h
      ______
     / ____/___  ___  _____________  _____
    / /   / __ \/ _ \/ ___/ ___/ _ \/ ___/
   / /___/ /_/ /  __/ /  / /__/  __/ /      v2.4.3
   \____/\____/\___/_/   \___/\___/_/       by @podalirius_
 
 usage: coercer [-h] [-v] {scan,coerce,fuzz} ...
 
 Automatic windows authentication coercer using various methods.
 
 positional arguments:
 {scan,coerce,fuzz}  Mode
   scan              Tests known methods with known working paths on all
                     methods, and report when an authentication is received.
   coerce            Trigger authentications through all known methods with
                     known working paths
   fuzz              Tests every method with a list of exploit paths, and
                     report when an authentication is received.
 
 options:
 -h, --help          show this help message and exit
 -v, --verbose       Verbose mode (default: False)

apt_metadata.description:
coerce a Windows server to authenticate on an arbitrary machine
A Python script to automatically coerce a Windows server to authenticate on an
arbitrary machine through many methods.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
